Guidelines on Directional Correlations of γ-rays from Oriented States (DCO)

1. General Selection Rules for Gamma Transitions

The conservation of angular momentum governs the allowed transitions between an initial nuclear state (JiJ_iJi​) and a final state (JfJ_fJf​) via radiation of multipolarity LLL:

∣Ji−Jf∣≤L≤Ji+Jf |J_i - J_f| \leq L \leq J_i + J_f ∣Ji​−Jf​∣≤L≤Ji​+Jf​
∣Ji−L∣≤Jf≤Ji+L|J_i - L| \leq J_f \leq J_i + L∣Ji​−L∣≤Jf​≤Ji​+L
  • Multipolarity (LLL): Dipole transitions correspond to L=1L=1L=1, while quadrupole transitions correspond to L=2L=2L=2.
  • Spin Change (ΔJ\Delta JΔJ): Defined as the absolute difference ∣Ji−Jf∣|J_i - J_f|∣Ji​−Jf​∣.
  • Vector Coupling: The radiation carries away angular momentum LLL, which must be the vector sum of the initial and final nuclear spins.

3. Classification of Transitions

Transitions are classified by the relationship between radiation multipolarity (LLL) and the change in nuclear spin (ΔJ=∣Ji−Jf∣\Delta J = |J_i - J_f|ΔJ=∣Ji​−Jf​∣).

Stretched Transitions (∣ΔJ∣=L|\Delta J| = L∣ΔJ∣=L)

The γ-ray carries the maximum angular momentum allowed by its multipole order. Angular momentum vectors of the initial state, final state, and photon are aligned.

  • Stretched Quadrupole (L=2L=2L=2): ΔJ=2\Delta J = 2ΔJ=2 (e.g., 4+→2+4^+ \to 2^+4+→2+).
  • Stretched Dipole (L=1L=1L=1): ΔJ=1\Delta J = 1ΔJ=1 (e.g., 4+→3+4^+ \to 3^+4+→3+).

Unstretched Transitions (∣ΔJ∣<L|\Delta J| < L∣ΔJ∣<L)

The nuclear spin changes by less than the multipole order. The angular momentum vectors are not fully aligned (vector reorientation).

  • For Dipole (L=1L=1L=1): Transition is unstretched if ΔJ=0\Delta J = 0ΔJ=0.
  • For Quadrupole (L=2L=2L=2): Transition is unstretched if ΔJ=1\Delta J = 1ΔJ=1 or ΔJ=0\Delta J = 0ΔJ=0.

2. Introduction to DCO Ratios

The DCO ratio (RDCOR_{DCO}RDCO​) is an experimental observable used to determine the multipolarity and electromagnetic character of γ-ray transitions. By comparing intensities measured at different angles relative to the beam axis, physicists can deduce the change in nuclear spin (ΔJ\Delta JΔJ) between energy levels.

The ratio is defined as:

RDCO=Iγ(θ1 gated at θ2)Iγ(θ2 gated at θ1) R_{DCO} = \frac{I_{\gamma}(\theta_1 \text{ gated at } \theta_2)}{I_{\gamma}(\theta_2 \text{ gated at } \theta_1)} RDCO​=Iγ​(θ2​ gated at θ1​)Iγ​(θ1​ gated at θ2​)​
  • Assumed geometry: θ1≈37∘\theta_1 \approx 37^\circθ1​≈37∘ and θ2≈79∘\theta_2 \approx 79^\circθ2​≈79∘.
  • Like-to-Like Gates: When gating on a transition of known multipolarity, the observed RDCOR_{DCO}RDCO​ values for the same multipolarity transitions are expected to be close to unity.

4. Interpretation Rules (ENSDF 2021 Guidelines)

A. Gate on Stretched Quadrupole Transition

Reference Transition: ΔJ=2\Delta J = 2ΔJ=2

Observed Transition Type Spin Change (ΔJ\Delta JΔJ) Expected RDCOR_{DCO}RDCO​
Stretched Quadrupole ΔJ=2\Delta J = 2ΔJ=2 ≈1.0\approx 1.0≈1.0
Stretched Dipole ΔJ=1\Delta J = 1ΔJ=1 ≈0.56\approx 0.56≈0.56
Mixed Dipole + Quadrupole ΔJ=1\Delta J = 1ΔJ=1 0.2 – 1.30.2 \text{ -- } 1.30.2 – 1.3 (Depends on mixing ratio δ\deltaδ)
Unstretched Dipole ΔJ=0\Delta J = 0ΔJ=0 ≈1.0\approx 1.0≈1.0
Mixed Unstretched ΔJ=0\Delta J = 0ΔJ=0 0.6 – 1.00.6 \text{ -- } 1.00.6 – 1.0

B. Gate on Stretched Dipole Transition

Reference Transition: ΔJ=1\Delta J = 1ΔJ=1.

Observed Transition Type Spin Change (ΔJ\Delta JΔJ) Expected RDCOR_{DCO}RDCO​
Stretched Quadrupole ΔJ=2\Delta J = 2ΔJ=2 ≈1.8\approx 1.8≈1.8
Stretched Dipole ΔJ=1\Delta J = 1ΔJ=1 ≈1.0\approx 1.0≈1.0
Unstretched Dipole ΔJ=0\Delta J = 0ΔJ=0 ≈1.8\approx 1.8≈1.8

C. Ambiguities

  • Overlap: An unstretched dipole transition (ΔJ=0\Delta J = 0ΔJ=0) exhibits similar RDCOR_{DCO}RDCO​ values to a stretched quadrupole transition (ΔJ=2\Delta J = 2ΔJ=2) in both gating configurations (1.01.01.0 in Quad gate; 1.81.81.8 in Dipole gate).
  • Resolution: Linear polarization measurements are required to distinguish these cases by determining the electromagnetic character (Electric vs. Magnetic).

5. Application Examples

Initial state spin: Ji=9/2J_i = 9/2Ji​=9/2

Classification Spin Change (ΔJ\Delta JΔJ) Final Spin (JfJ_fJf​)
Stretched Quadrupole 222 5/25/25/2 or 13/213/213/2
Unstretched Quadrupole 111 7/27/27/2 or 11/211/211/2
Unstretched Quadrupole 000 9/29/29/2
Stretched Dipole 111 7/27/27/2 or 11/211/211/2
Unstretched Dipole 000 9/29/29/2
